title: salix lanata subsp. richardsonii
intro: Salix lanata subsp. richardsonii, commonly known as Richardson's willow, is a subspecies of the Salix lanata plant, a type of willow tree native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with Salix lanata subsp. richardsonii include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Richardson's willow include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
